key_insight: A mesh graph neural network can predict 2D von Mises stress fields for plates with different hole geometries, generalizing better than conventional ML models because it uses mesh connectivity, node types, and relative geometry instead of absolute coordinates.
engineering_relevance: This offers a fast surrogate for finite element analysis, making iterative structural design and load screening more practical when many geometry variants must be evaluated.
method: Mesh graph network on finite-element meshes
Inputs: node type, relative edge distances, and global load
evidence_signal: Trained on 11 plate geometries and 20 load cases, then tested on 7 unseen geometries and 3 unseen loads, with the best case reaching R² ≥ 0.97 versus about 0.01–0.86 for Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and KNN.
watch_item: Performance is strongest in some unseen cases but still variable, so deployment should check accuracy carefully on geometries or loads far outside the training distribution.
paper_title: Mesh Graph Neural Network Framework for Accelerating Finite Element Simulation for Arbitrary Geometries
